declaredLane: Presentation and Domain anti-pattern cleanup with recurrence prevention
observedMutationClass: presentation ViewModel data shape, catalog alignment grouping, presenter helper extraction, domain value object grouping, domain invariants, injected status rules, and guardrail documentation
acceptanceEvidenceClass: C# compile, feature presentation lint, and rules lint
rootCause: Garage presentation code allowed large flat ViewModel/catalog data shapes and long compatibility constructors to remain on production paths, while domain entities accepted primitive stat sets without a grouped invariant owner. Status container policy lookup also depended directly on the static rule class, which made rule ownership hard to substitute.
prevention: Production presentation paths now use structured display/preview and alignment group objects instead of flat data clumps. Domain Unit construction now groups part ids, combat stats, and energy costs with validation, and StatusContainer accepts a rule set boundary. Guardrails now require grouped value objects for large data shapes, structured ViewModel constructors for new production code, presenter boundary checks for domain calculations, immutable value-object style updates, constructor invariants, and injected rule owners. This change preserves the existing hard-fail coverage for active plan artifact owner shape drift, active plan concrete artifact owner collision, and progress evidence overload.
verification: dotnet build Assembly-CSharp.csproj --no-restore -v:minimal; dotnet build Assembly-CSharp-Editor.csproj --no-restore -v:minimal; npm run --silent feature:presentation:lint; npm run --silent rules:lint
